footer.description: Your platform for digital income from content creation and education.
footer.address: Georgia, Tbilisi, Leo and Nodar Gabuniya II Lane, No. 3, Apt. 27

aboutUs.title: About Us
aboutUs.subtitle: Swavleba - Your Income Platform from Digital Content
aboutUs.introText: Swavleba was created with one main idea: knowledge sharing and content creation should be a source of income for everyone. We combine learning, content creation, and monetization on one platform where users can learn from lecturers, create video clips, and earn money from their own creations.
aboutUs.missionText: Our mission is to create an ecosystem where everyone can earn income from their skills and creativity. Whether you want to learn new skills, create your own content, or share professional lecturer courses - Swavleba is one platform for all opportunities.
aboutUs.forUsers1: Unified space: learning, content creation, and earning on one platform, in just a few clicks.
aboutUs.forUsers2: Quality educational courses: from professional lecturers in various directions.
aboutUs.forUsers3: Monetization opportunity: earn income from your created clips.
aboutUs.forUsers5: Security: protected payments and reliable financial operations.
aboutUs.forLecturers1: Attract new audiences: the platform increases visibility and expands the student base.
aboutUs.forLecturers2: Additional income: earn income from clip views.
aboutUs.forLecturers3: Efficient management: quick course uploads and control.
aboutUs.forLecturers4: Marketing support: promotions, campaigns, and direct communication with students.
aboutUs.forLecturers5: Reliability: transparent terms and secure settlements.
aboutUs.howItWorks1: Learn: choose your desired course and learn from professionals.
aboutUs.howItWorks2: Create: cut out the best moments, create useful clips.
aboutUs.howItWorks3: Publish: upload your clips to the platform.
aboutUs.howItWorks4: Earn: receive income from every view.
aboutUs.whyWavleba1: Because we combine learning and earning on one platform.
aboutUs.whyWavleba2: Because we care about both student development and lecturer success.
aboutUs.whyWavleba3: Because our goal is to give everyone the opportunity to earn money from their own creativity.
aboutUs.visionText: Today Swavleba offers users educational courses and the opportunity to create clips, but this is just the beginning. We plan to expand the platform in various directions - podcasts, live streams, masterclasses, workshops, and much more. We believe that Swavleba will soon become a platform where everyone can learn, create, and earn - don't think, create and earn!

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.s1t1: 1.1. These Terms and Conditions (hereinafter — "Agreement") regulate the relationship between the Swavleba platform (hereinafter — "Platform", "We") and its users (hereinafter — "User", "You").
terms.s1info:
Full name: Swavleba
Legal form: Individual entrepreneur, small business status (Chapter XII of the Georgian Tax Code)
Address: Georgia, Tbilisi, Leo and Nodar Gabuniya II Lane, No. 3, Apt. 27
Email: bejisscience@gmail.com
Phone: +995 555 54 99 88
terms.s1t3: 1.3. The Platform is an electronic intermediary service that connects lecturers (content creators) and students (users). The Platform is not an educational institution and does not carry out educational activities within the meaning of Georgian legislation.
terms.s1t4: 1.4. The Platform operates in accordance with Georgian legislation, including: the Civil Code of Georgia; the Law of Georgia on Electronic Commerce (No. 3110, 2023); the Law of Georgia on Consumer Rights Protection (No. 1455, 2022); the Law of Georgia on Personal Data Protection (No. 3144, 2023).
terms.s1t5: 1.5. By registering on the Platform, using services, or browsing the website, you agree to all terms of this Agreement. If you do not agree to any term, please discontinue use of the Platform.
terms.s2intro: 2.1. Terms used in this Agreement have the following meanings:
terms.s2i1: "Platform" — the website swavleba.ge and all related services, applications, and digital infrastructure.
terms.s2i2: "Student" — a natural person registered on the Platform for learning purposes.
terms.s2i3: "Lecturer" — a natural or legal person who creates and publishes educational courses on the Platform.
terms.s2i4: "Course" — a set of educational materials created by a lecturer, including video lectures and supplementary materials.
terms.s2i5: "Bundle" — a package of two or more courses sold at a discounted price.
terms.s2i6: "Project" — a video assignment created by a lecturer, within which students create and distribute video content on social media.
terms.s2i7: "RPM (Revenue Per Mille)" — the payment rate per 1,000 views, set by the lecturer for project criteria.
terms.s2i8: "Referral code" — a user's unique identifier used to attract new users.
terms.s2i9: "Balance" — a user's virtual account on the Platform, reflecting received and withdrawn amounts.
terms.s2i10: "Payment provider" — Keepz — a payment service provider registered with the National Bank of Georgia.
terms.s2i11: "Commission" — the payment provider's commission and/or the Platform's service commission.
terms.s3t1: 3.1. The Platform operates as a technological intermediary service in accordance with Articles 11–14 of the Law of Georgia on Electronic Commerce. The Platform does not exercise editorial control over content published by lecturers.
terms.s3t2: 3.2. The Platform is not responsible for the quality, completeness, or outcomes of courses. The lecturer is fully responsible for the content and quality of their own materials.
terms.s3t3: 3.3. The Platform does not guarantee that a student will acquire specific skills, receive a certificate, or achieve any particular outcome by completing a course.
terms.s3t4: 3.4. In the case of projects, the Platform provides intermediation between the project creator (lecturer) and the performer (student). The Platform assumes no responsibility for the quality, content, or outcomes of the created video content.
terms.s3t5: 3.5. The Platform acts as the lecturer's limited payment agent — payment made by the student to the payment provider (Keepz) is considered fulfillment of the obligation to the lecturer.
terms.s4t1: 4.1. To register on the Platform, the following is required: email address, password (minimum 6 characters), and username (3-30 characters). Alternatively, authorization via Google account is available.
terms.s4t2: 4.2. Upon registration, the user selects a role: student or lecturer. Lecturer status requires administration approval.
terms.s4t3: 4.3. The user is obligated to provide accurate and complete information during registration and ensure it remains current.
terms.s4t4: 4.4. The user is fully responsible for the security of their account, maintaining password confidentiality, and any actions performed within the account.
terms.s4t5: 4.5. One natural person is entitled to have only one account. Creating duplicate accounts is prohibited.
terms.s4t6: 4.6. The Platform reserves the right to refuse registration or suspend/cancel an account if there is reasonable suspicion that the user is violating the terms of this Agreement.
terms.s5t1: 5.1. Lecturers independently create, set prices for, and manage their own courses. The Platform does not interfere with course content creation.
terms.s5t2: 5.2. Course prices are listed in Georgian Lari (GEL / ₾). The price includes all fees, except the payment provider's commission, which may additionally be charged to the user depending on the payment method.
terms.s5t3: 5.3. After enrollment, the student receives unlimited access to the course, unless a specific access period is indicated for the particular course.
terms.s5t4: 5.4. By enrolling in a bundle, the student receives access to all courses included in the bundle.
terms.s5t5: 5.5. The lecturer retains full intellectual property of their content. The Platform is granted a non-exclusive, global, royalty-free license to display, distribute, and promote the content for the purpose of Platform operation and service delivery.
terms.s6t1: 6.1. Enrollment in a course/bundle is processed through the payment provider (Keepz). Upon successful payment, enrollment occurs automatically.
terms.s6t2: 6.2. Payment methods: The Platform, through Keepz, accepts the following payment methods: bank cards (Visa, Mastercard); internet banking (TBC, Bank of Georgia, Credo, Liberty); mobile payments (Apple Pay, Google Pay); cryptocurrency (Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDT).
terms.s6t3: 6.3. Payment process: 6.3.1. The user initiates payment — the system creates an order in Keepz. 6.3.2. The user is redirected to the Keepz payment page or pays with a saved card. 6.3.3. After successful payment, Keepz notifies the Platform and enrollment occurs automatically. 6.3.4. The payment order expires after 5 (five) minutes if payment is not completed.
terms.s6t4: 6.4. Commission: 6.4.1. Each transaction incurs: (a) payment provider (Keepz) commission; (b) Platform service commission. 6.4.2. Commission amounts may change at any time. Both parties (lecturer and student) will be informed in advance, within a reasonable timeframe. 6.4.3. In accordance with Article 22 of the Law on Consumer Rights Protection, in case of price changes, the consumer has the right to refuse the service.
terms.s6t5: 6.5. Order statuses: Pending — payment/administration approval is in progress; Approved — access has been granted; Rejected — enrollment was denied with reason stated.
terms.s7t1: 7.1. In accordance with Article 13 of the Law of Georgia on Consumer Rights Protection, the consumer has the right to withdraw from a distance contract within 14 (fourteen) calendar days without stating a reason.
terms.s7t2: 7.2. In accordance with Article 14, the right of withdrawal does not apply to: (a) services that have been fully performed with the consumer's prior, active consent and the consumer's acknowledgment that they lose the right of withdrawal; (b) digital content whose delivery has begun with the consumer's prior, active consent.
terms.s7t3: 7.3. Upon course enrollment, the student actively agrees to receive immediate access to the course and acknowledges that they thereby lose the 14-day right of withdrawal.
terms.s7t4: 7.4. To exercise the right of withdrawal, the user must contact the Platform via email: bejisscience@gmail.com. Refunds are processed in accordance with the Refund Policy.
terms.s8Title: Article 8. Projects and Video Content Monetization
terms.s8t1: 8.1. Project essence: The lecturer creates a project within which students create and distribute video content on social media (TikTok, Instagram). The project has a budget, criteria, and validity period.
terms.s8t2: 8.2. Compensation formula: Student compensation is calculated using the following formula: Compensation = (Confirmed view count ÷ 1,000) × RPM, where RPM (Rate Per Mille) is the compensation rate per 1,000 views set by the lecturer for each satisfied criterion.
terms.s8t3: 8.3. View counting: 8.3.1. Video view counts are tracked by an automated system (bot) that retrieves data from TikTok and Instagram platforms. 8.3.2. The Platform assumes no responsibility for the accuracy of view counts provided by the automated system. 8.3.3. In case of termination or restriction of access to third-party platforms, the Platform is not obligated to provide alternative view counting methods.
terms.s8t4: 8.4. Budget depletion: 8.4.1. In case of project budget depletion, the remaining amount will be distributed according to views accumulated at the time of depletion. 8.4.2. No compensation will be issued for views accumulated after budget depletion.
terms.s9t1: 9.1. Project access is available through three methods: (a) Course enrollment — unlimited access to that specific course's projects; (b) Upon registration — 1-month free access to all projects; (c) Paid subscription — 1-month access.
terms.s9t2: 9.2. Subscription price (currently ~₾10/month) may change at any time. Price changes do not affect already active subscriptions.
terms.s9t3: 9.3. Subscriptions do not auto-renew. After expiration, the user must purchase a new subscription.
terms.s10t1: 10.1. Every registered user receives a unique referral code that can be shared to earn commission.
terms.s10t2: 10.2. The referral commission percentage is set by the lecturer for each course (0% to 100%). The Platform has no involvement in determining the commission rate.
terms.s10t3: 10.3. Commission calculation: Commission = (Lecturer-set percentage ÷ 100) × Course price.
terms.s10t4: 10.4. Referral rules: (a) Self-referral is prohibited; (b) Only one referral applies per enrollment; (c) Referral does not apply on re-enrollment; (d) Commission is credited after enrollment approval.
terms.s11t1: 11.1. Funds can be accumulated on the Platform through: referral commission, course sales (for lecturers), project compensation (for students).
terms.s11t2: 11.2. Withdrawal conditions: (a) Minimum amount — ₾50 (fifty Lari); (b) A Georgian bank account IBAN is required (format: GE + 2 digits + 2 letters + 16 digits = 22 characters); (c) Only one withdrawal request may be submitted at a time.
terms.s11t3: 11.3. Withdrawal requests are processed within a maximum of 5 (five) business days from the request date.
terms.s11t4: 11.4. The administration reserves the right to refuse withdrawal with reason stated. In case of refusal, the amount is returned to the user's balance.
terms.s12t1: 12.1. The user is obligated to: (a) provide accurate and complete information; (b) use the Platform only for lawful purposes; (c) respect the rights of other users; (d) not create fake accounts; (e) not use automated systems (bots) for unauthorized access to the Platform.
terms.s12t2: 12.2. Prohibited actions: fake transactions; unauthorized data usage; posting malicious content; reverse engineering of Platform infrastructure; spreading hate speech, discriminatory, or violence-inciting content; misleading other users; artificially inflating views (fake views).
terms.s13t1: 13.1. The Platform's brand, logo, design, software code, and other technical infrastructure are the intellectual property of Swavleba.
terms.s13t2: 13.2. The lecturer retains full intellectual property of their content (videos, materials, course structure). The Platform is granted a non-exclusive license for the purpose of service delivery.
terms.s13t3: 13.3. Intellectual property of video content created by a student within a project belongs to the student creator.
terms.s13t4: 13.4. Users are prohibited from copying, distributing, modifying, or commercially using Platform content without the Platform's written consent.
terms.s14t1: 14.1. The Platform provides services on an "as-is" basis. The Platform does not guarantee uninterrupted service, error-free operation, or fitness for particular purposes.
terms.s14t2: 14.2. The Platform's maximum liability for any claim or demand is limited to: (a) ₾100 (one hundred Lari); or (b) the amount paid by the user in the last 12 months — whichever is greater.
terms.s14t3: 14.3. The Platform is not liable for: (a) indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages; (b) lost income; (c) data loss; (d) business interruption.
terms.s14t4: 14.4. In accordance with Article 395 of the Civil Code of Georgia, the limitation of liability does not apply to intentional acts or gross negligence that caused harm to health or life.
terms.s14t5: 14.5. The Platform is not liable for service interruption or termination due to force majeure circumstances. Force majeure includes: natural disasters, war, epidemics, government decisions, internet infrastructure failures, changes in third-party platform (TikTok, Instagram) availability.
terms.s15t1: 15.1. The Platform is authorized to suspend or cancel an account in the following cases: (a) violation of these Terms and Conditions; (b) fraudulent activity or attempted fraud; (c) violation of other users' rights; (d) account inactivity for more than 3 months; (e) violation of legislation.
terms.s15t2: 15.2. Before account cancellation, except in urgent cases, the Platform will notify the user within a reasonable timeframe and provide an opportunity for explanation.
terms.s15t3: 15.3. The user has the right to delete their account at any time. In case of account deletion, data is retained for the period provided by Georgian legislation.
terms.s15t4: 15.4. The balance existing at the time of account cancellation will be disbursed through the standard withdrawal procedure, provided the user has not violated essential terms of the Agreement.
terms.s16t1: 16.1. Collection and processing of personal data is carried out in accordance with the Privacy Policy and the Law of Georgia on Personal Data Protection.
terms.s16t2: 16.2. Detailed information about data processing is provided in a separate document — the Privacy Policy (swavleba.ge/privacy-policy).
terms.s17Title: Article 17. Changes to Terms and Conditions
terms.s17t1: 17.1. The Platform is authorized to change these Terms and Conditions at any time.
terms.s17t2: 17.2. Users will be informed of significant changes via email and/or platform notification, within a reasonable timeframe before the changes take effect.
terms.s17t3: 17.3. Continued use of the Platform after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
terms.s17t4: 17.4. If the user does not agree to changes, they have the right to discontinue use of the Platform and request account deletion.
terms.s18Title: Article 18. Applicable Law and Dispute Resolution
terms.s18t1: 18.1. This Agreement is governed by Georgian legislation.
terms.s18t2: 18.2. Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement must first be resolved through negotiation (within 30 calendar days).
terms.s18t3: 18.3. In case of unsuccessful negotiation, the parties may resort to mediation.
terms.s18t4: 18.4. The user may also file a complaint with the Competition and Consumer Protection Agency of Georgia (GCCA).
terms.s18t5: 18.5. In case of unsuccessful mediation, the dispute shall be resolved in Tbilisi City Court.
terms.s18t6: 18.6. In accordance with Article 22(3)(s) of the Law on Consumer Rights Protection, restricting the consumer's legal remedies through standard terms is impermissible.
terms.s19t1: 19.1. If any provision of the Agreement is found to be invalid, the remaining provisions remain in effect.
terms.s19t2: 19.2. In accordance with Article 21(3) of the Law on Consumer Rights Protection, ambiguous terms of the Agreement shall be interpreted in favor of the consumer.
terms.s19t3: 19.3. Failure by the Platform to exercise any right does not constitute a waiver of that right.
terms.s19t4: 19.4. Language of the Agreement — Georgian. Currency — Georgian Lari (₾ / GEL).

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Text1: 1.1. This Privacy Policy (hereinafter — "Policy") defines how Swavleba collects, uses, stores, and protects your personal data.
privacy.section1Text2: 1.2. The Policy is prepared in accordance with Georgia's Law on Personal Data Protection (No. 3144, 2023), effective from March 1, 2024.
privacy.section1Text3: 1.3. By using the platform, you agree to data processing in accordance with this Policy.
privacy.section2Text:
Company: Swavleba
Legal form: Individual entrepreneur, small business status
Address: Georgia, Tbilisi, Leo and Nodar Gabuniya II Lane, No. 3, Apt. 27
Email for data protection issues: bejisscience@gmail.com
Phone: +995 555 54 99 88
privacy.section3Sub1Text: Email address, username, password (encrypted), selected role (student/lecturer), Google account identifier (for Google OAuth).
privacy.section3Sub2Text: Payment data (bank card number, expiration) is processed exclusively by the payment provider (Keepz). Swavleba stores only: last 4 digits of the card (masked), brand, expiration, and transaction identifier.
privacy.section3Sub3Text: IP address, device type and operating system, browser type and version, cookies, session data, platform usage statistics.
privacy.section3Sub4Text: Uploaded videos and materials (for lecturers), video links submitted for projects (for students), chat messages, ratings and reviews.
privacy.section3Sub5Text: View counts, likes, comments, shares, and saves of videos submitted within projects (TikTok, Instagram), collected by an automated system.
privacy.section3Sub6Text: Balance, transaction history, IBAN (for withdrawals), referral code and commission history.
privacy.section4Title: Article 4. Purposes and Legal Bases for Data Processing
privacy.section4Item1: Contract performance (Article 5(b)): registration, account management, course access, payment processing, balance management, withdrawals.
privacy.section4Item2: Consent (Article 5(a)): Google Analytics and analytical cookies, marketing communication, optional notifications.
privacy.section4Item3: Legitimate interest (Article 5(f)): platform security, fraud prevention, technical problem detection, service improvement.
privacy.section4Item4: Legal obligation (Article 5(c)): tax accounting, financial reporting, cooperation with law enforcement.
privacy.section5Text1: 5.1. Swavleba does not sell your personal data to third parties.
privacy.section5Text2: 5.2. Data may be shared with: (a) Keepz — payment service provider (for payment processing); (b) hosting and cloud service providers (for data storage); (c) Google Analytics (for analytics, with your consent); (d) law enforcement agencies (only in cases provided by law).
privacy.section5Text3: 5.3. Lecturers will have access only to limited information about their course students (username, enrollment status).
privacy.section6Item1: 6.1. Account data — account active period + 3 years (statute of limitations).
privacy.section6Item2: 6.2. Tax/financial records — 6 years (Georgian Tax Code requirement).
privacy.section6Item4: 6.4. Cookies — according to consent period (maximum 12 months).
privacy.section6Item5: 6.5. After account deletion, data is retained only for the period required by law, then deleted or anonymized.
privacy.section7Item1: 7.1. Encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+) for all communications.
privacy.section7Item2: 7.2. Encryption at rest for databases and backups.
privacy.section8Intro: 8.1. In accordance with the Law on Personal Data Protection, you have the following rights:
privacy.section8Item1: Right to information — receive information about the processing of your data.
privacy.section8Item2: Right of access — request a copy of data processed about you.
privacy.section8Item3: Right to rectification — request correction of inaccurate data.
privacy.section8Item4: Right to erasure — request deletion of data ("right to be forgotten").
privacy.section8Item5: Right to restriction — request restriction of data processing.
privacy.section8Item6: Right to portability — receive your data in a structured format.
privacy.section8Item7: Right to object — object to data processing.
privacy.section8Item8: Right regarding automated decisions — not be subject to solely automated decisions.
privacy.section8Contact: 8.2. To exercise your rights, contact: bejisscience@gmail.com
privacy.section8Response: 8.3. We will respond to your request within 10 business days (in exceptional cases — an additional 10 business days).
privacy.section8Complaint: 8.4. If you believe your data is being processed incorrectly, you have the right to file a complaint with the Personal Data Protection Service (pdps.ge).
privacy.section9Intro: 9.1. The platform uses cookies in three categories:
privacy.section9Item1: Essential cookies — required for platform functionality (authorization, session). Legal basis: legitimate interest.
privacy.section9Item2: Analytical cookies — Google Analytics, platform usage statistics. Legal basis: consent.
privacy.section9Item3: Functional cookies — language preference, interface settings. Legal basis: consent.
privacy.section9Text: 9.2. Consent for analytical and functional cookies is requested on first visit. You can change your consent at any time from browser settings.
privacy.section10Text1: 10.1. The platform is not intended for persons under 18 without the consent of a parent or legal guardian.
privacy.section10Text2: 10.2. In accordance with Article 7 of the Law on Personal Data Protection, processing data of persons under 16 requires the consent of a parent/legal guardian.
privacy.section11Text1: 11.1. If your data is transferred outside Georgia (e.g., cloud hosting), this occurs only to countries or organizations that ensure adequate data protection.
privacy.section11Text2: 11.2. Transfers to EU member states do not require additional protection measures, as the EU provides an adequate level of protection.
privacy.section12Text1: 12.1. Swavleba is authorized to change this Policy at any time.
privacy.section12Text2: 12.2. Users will be informed of changes via email and/or platform notification.
privacy.section12Text3: 12.3. The updated Policy takes effect immediately upon publication.
privacy.contactInfo:

Email: bejisscience@gmail.com
Phone: +995 555 54 99 88
Website: swavleba.ge

personalSecurity.title: Personal Information Security Policy
personalSecurity.section1Text1: 1.1. This policy defines the principles, measures, and procedures for protecting personal information by the Swavleba platform.
personalSecurity.section1Text2: 1.2. The policy is prepared in accordance with Article 27 (Data Security) and Article 26 (Confidentiality by Design/Default) of Georgia's Law on Personal Data Protection (No. 3144, 2023).
personalSecurity.section2Item1: 2.1. Registration data: email, username, encrypted password.
personalSecurity.section2Item2: 2.2. Payment data: processed through Keepz, only masked card data is stored on the platform.
personalSecurity.section2Item3: 2.3. Technical data: IP address, device information, session data.
personalSecurity.section2Item4: 2.4. Location data: approximate (from IP address), precise (GPS — only with consent).
personalSecurity.section2Item5: 2.5. Content data: videos, chat messages, reviews.
personalSecurity.section2Item6: 2.6. Financial data: balance, transaction history, IBAN.
personalSecurity.section3Item1: Lawfulness — data processing occurs only on legal grounds provided by law.
personalSecurity.section3Item2: Data minimization — we collect only the data necessary to achieve a specific purpose.
personalSecurity.section3Item3: Transparency — we clearly inform users what data we collect and why.
personalSecurity.section3Item4: Accuracy — we strive to ensure data accuracy and provide users with the ability to correct it.
personalSecurity.section3Item5: Storage limitation — data is stored only for the period necessary to achieve the purpose.
personalSecurity.section3Item6: Integrity and confidentiality — data is protected from unauthorized access, loss, and damage.
personalSecurity.section3Item7: Accountability — we record all data processing operations and verify compliance.
personalSecurity.section4Sub1Text: In transit: TLS 1.2+ for all communications (HTTPS). At rest: AES-256 encryption for databases and backups. Passwords: hashed with bcrypt algorithm, never stored in plain text.
personalSecurity.section4Sub2Text: Role-based access control system (RBAC): student, lecturer, administrator. Principle of least privilege — each user has access only to the data necessary for their role.
personalSecurity.section4Sub3Text: Continuous monitoring of system logs. Automatic detection of suspicious activity. Storage of access logs for audit purposes.
personalSecurity.section4Sub4Text: Regular backup creation. Encrypted backup storage. Regular testing of recovery procedures.
personalSecurity.section4Sub5Text: Firewall and intrusion detection systems. DDoS protection. Regular security updates.
personalSecurity.section5Item1: Request a copy of your data;
personalSecurity.section5Contact: Contact: bejisscience@gmail.com. Response: within 10 business days.
personalSecurity.section6Item1: 6.1. Incident detection and assessment — within 24 hours.
personalSecurity.section6Item2: 6.2. Notification to the Personal Data Protection Service (PDPS) — within 72 hours of incident detection (in accordance with Article 29 of the Law).
personalSecurity.section6Item3: 6.3. Informing affected users — immediately, in plain and understandable language, if the incident poses a significant threat to their rights.
personalSecurity.section6Item4: 6.4. Taking corrective measures and documenting the incident.
personalSecurity.section6Item5: 6.5. Maintaining an incident register (record of all incidents, consequences, and measures taken).
personalSecurity.section6Item6: 6.6. Upon request of the Personal Data Protection Service, submission of processing operation records within 3 business days (Article 28 of the Law).
personalSecurity.section7Text1: 7.1. The policy will be updated as needed, at least once a year.
personalSecurity.section7Text2: 7.2. Users will be informed of significant changes.
personalSecurity.contactInfo:
Company: Swavleba

Address: Georgia, Tbilisi, Leo and Nodar Gabuniya II Lane, No. 3, Apt. 27
Email: bejisscience@gmail.com
Phone: +995 555 54 99 88
Website: swavleba.ge

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.section1Text1: 1.1. This policy defines the conditions and procedure for refunds of services purchased on the Swavleba platform.
refund.section1Text2: 1.2. The policy is prepared in accordance with Articles 13–15 of Georgia's Law on Consumer Rights Protection (No. 1455, 2022).
refund.section1Text3: 1.3. The platform is an intermediary service connecting lecturers and students. The platform is responsible only for the technical delivery of the service, not for the quality of course content or learning outcomes.
refund.section2Text1: 2.1. In accordance with Article 13 of the Law, the consumer has the right to withdraw from a distance contract within 14 calendar days, without stating a reason.
refund.section2Text2: 2.2. The 14-day period starts from the moment of enrollment in a course/bundle.
refund.section2Text3: 2.3. Important exception (Article 14 of the Law): if upon enrollment the student agreed to receive immediate access to the course and acknowledged losing the right of withdrawal — the 14-day refund period no longer applies.
refund.section3Intro: 3.1. A full refund is provided in the following cases:
refund.section3Item1: Technical failure — the service could not be delivered due to a platform technical problem (e.g., inability to access the course due to platform fault).
refund.section3Item2: Force majeure — service delivery is impossible due to force majeure circumstances.
refund.section3Item3: Platform/partner fault — the service was not delivered due to the fault of the platform or lecturer (e.g., the course was deleted shortly after enrollment).
refund.section3Item4: Double payment — the same service was paid for twice due to a technical error.
refund.section4Intro: 4.1. Refunds are not provided in the following cases:
refund.section4Item1: (a) The student has already received access to the course and agreed to waive the right of withdrawal (Article 2.3);
refund.section4Item2: (b) The project subscription period has expired or the service was fully delivered;
refund.section4Item3: (c) The user violated the terms and conditions and their account was terminated;
refund.section4Item4: (d) Claims related to the quality of course content (the lecturer, not the platform, is responsible for quality);
refund.section4Item5: (e) Failure to achieve learning outcomes by the student (the platform does not guarantee learning outcomes).
refund.section5Text1: 5.1. To request a refund, the user must send an email to: bejisscience@gmail.com
refund.section5Text2: 5.2. The request must include: full name; registered email address; purchase/transaction details; reason for refund; payment confirmation document (optional).
refund.section5Text3: 5.3. The request will be reviewed within 10 (ten) business days.
refund.section5Text4: 5.4. If approved, the refund will be made using the same payment method as the original payment (in accordance with Article 15(2)(a) of the Law), unless the consumer agrees otherwise.
refund.section5Text5: 5.5. The actual refund will be processed within 14 calendar days of the decision.
refund.section6Text1: 6.1. The commission charged by the payment provider (Keepz) may not be refunded, as this amount is the payment provider's service fee that has already been paid.
refund.section6Text2: 6.2. If the payment provider refunds the commission, this amount will also be returned to the user.
refund.section7Text1: 7.1. A project budget funded by a lecturer is not refundable if the project is active and students are already receiving compensation.
refund.section7Text2: 7.2. Refund of unspent budget is possible only with administration approval.
refund.section8Text: 8.1. If a refund is denied, the user has the right to: (a) request a re-review from the platform; (b) file a complaint with the Georgian Competition and Consumer Protection Agency (GCCA); (c) apply to Tbilisi City Court.
refund.contactInfo:

Address: Georgia, Tbilisi, Leo and Nodar Gabuniya II Lane, No. 3, Apt. 27
